updates.title: Goal Action: Now with Support for User Replies!
updates.content:
**Goal Action: Trigger on Team Member Reply**

Automations can now detect when a team member replies to a contact and use that as a Goal event — immediately halting or redirecting the automation the moment human engagement begins.

**What's new:**
- "User Replied" is now a supported Goal event inside Kayan Automations
- When a team member sends a reply through any connected conversation channel, the contact jumps directly to the Goal step — skipping all intermediate actions
- Prevents contacts from receiving automated follow-ups after a real conversation has started
- Works across nurture sequences, support workflows, and any automation where human handoff matters

**How to set it up:**
1. Open the **Kayan Automations** tab and select the workflow you want to update
2. Add a **Goal** action at the point where human engagement should take over
3. Set the goal event to **User Replied**
4. Configure any actions that should run once the Goal is reached (e.g. move to a pipeline stage, add a tag, end the sequence)
5. Save and publish the workflow

**Notes:**
- The Goal triggers only when a team member sends the reply — not the contact
- Contacts skip all steps between their current position and the Goal step instantly
- Best used at the end of nurture sequences or before any actions that would create duplicate outreach

updates.title: Wait Step: Now with Support for User Replies!
updates.content:
**Wait Step Now Supports User Reply as a Wait Condition**

The Wait step in Kayan Automations now supports "User Replied" as a wait condition — letting you pause a workflow until a team member responds to a conversation, not just until a contact replies.

**What's new:**
- New wait condition: pause workflow execution until a user (team member) sends a reply in the conversation
- Optional timeout setting — if no reply is sent within your defined window, the workflow moves forward automatically
- On timeout, route contacts through escalation logic: supervisor alerts, conversation reassignments, task creation, or reminders
- If a user replies before the timeout, the workflow resumes immediately
- Works without a timeout configured — workflow simply waits indefinitely until a user reply is detected

**How to set it up:**

1. Open your workflow in the Kayan Automations tab and add a **Wait** step at the point where a team member response is expected.
2. Set the wait condition to **User Replied**.
3. Optionally define a timeout duration (e.g. 15 or 30 minutes) to cap the wait window.
4. Configure the path for when a reply is received, then configure a separate path for when the timeout expires — add escalation actions like notifications, reassignments, or task creation on the timeout branch.
5. Save and publish your workflow.

**Good for:** first-response SLA enforcement, sales lead response tracking, support escalation workflows, and internal response-time automations.

updates.title: Workflow Trigger: User Replied
updates.content:
**New Trigger: User Replied**

Automate actions the moment a team member responds to a contact — no more waiting on customer activity to move things forward.

**What's new:**
- A new automation trigger fires whenever a team member sends a reply to a contact
- Works across supported conversation channels
- Supports trigger filters so you control exactly when the automation runs
- Pairs with any follow-up actions: tagging, stage updates, internal notifications, task creation, assignments, and more

**How to set it up:**
1. Go to the **Automations** tab in your Kayan dashboard and open or create a workflow
2. Add the **User Replied** trigger
3. Configure any filters to narrow when the workflow should fire
4. Publish the workflow — it will run automatically each time a team member sends a reply

**Example use cases:**
- A rep replies to a new lead's SMS → opportunity stage updates to *Contacted* and the sales manager gets notified
- A support agent replies to a customer email → a *Support Responded* tag is applied and a 48-hour follow-up task is created

**Note:** This trigger activates on team member replies only, not incoming customer messages. Use it to build automations that respond to what your team does, not just what your contacts do.

updates.title: 💳 Services (v2): Payment Enhancements
updates.content:
**Services Payments: More Flexibility at Checkout**

Checkout for bookable services now supports flexible payment options, smarter deposit calculations, and better Buy Now, Pay Later compatibility.

**What's new:**

- **Deposit or full payment choice** — You can now enable an option that lets customers choose at checkout whether to pay a deposit or the full amount. Requires online payments to be active and a deposit configured on the service.
- **BNPL on full amounts** — Customers using Buy Now, Pay Later can now finance the complete service cost, not just the deposit, for a smoother booking experience.
- **Smarter percentage-based deposits** — Deposit amounts are now calculated against the final payable amount after discounts, add-ons, and taxes are applied. If a coupon is added, removed, or changed during checkout, the deposit recalculates in real time.
- **Flat deposits unchanged** — Fixed deposit amounts continue to work exactly as before.

---

**How to set it up:**

1. In your Kayan dashboard, go to **Services → Settings → Payment Settings**.
2. Set the payment mode to **Online Payment**.
3. Toggle on **Allow Deposit or Full Payment Choice**.
4. Save your changes.

Once enabled, customers booking services with a deposit configured will see both payment options at checkout.

updates.title: 🗓️ Buffer Time is Now Visible in Calendar Day & Week Views
updates.content:
**Buffer Time Now Visible in Day & Week Calendar Views**

Pre- and post-buffer times are now displayed directly in your Day and Week calendar views, giving you a clearer picture of how your schedule is spaced without changing how bookings work.

**What's new:**
- Buffer periods appear as distinct visual blocks before and after appointments in both Day and Week views
- Buffers are clearly differentiated from actual appointments so your schedule is easy to read at a glance
- Visibility applies across all calendar filters — by staff member, service, or category/group

**How to set it up:**
1. Open the **Calendar** section in your Kayan dashboard
2. Switch to **Day** or **Week** view
3. Enable the **Show buffer time** toggle
4. Buffer periods will appear highlighted around your appointments across all active filters

updates.title: Ad Manager: Opportunity Score for Meta Campaigns
updates.content:
**Ad Manager: Opportunity Score for Meta Campaigns**

Before you publish a Meta ad campaign, Kayan now shows you an Opportunity Score — a readiness rating that flags setup gaps and points you directly to where you can fix them.

**What's new:**

- **Pre-publish readiness score** — See a percentage score (rated Low, Medium, or High) on the campaign Review screen before going live
- **Improvement suggestions across four areas:**
  - *Audience* — flags if targeting is too narrow, too broad, or incomplete
  - *Budget* — identifies if spend settings may limit delivery or learning
  - *Creative* — reviews ad content, media, CTAs, and destination links
  - *Tracking* — checks whether conversion tracking is configured
- **Click-to-fix navigation** — each recommendation links directly to the relevant section of the campaign builder so you can make updates without hunting through settings
- **Prioritised guidance** — the score panel surfaces the highest-impact fixes first

**A few things to note:**
- Opportunity Score is currently available for Meta campaigns only
- It's a guidance tool — it does not guarantee performance or make changes automatically
- You decide which recommendations to act on

---

**How to set it up:**

1. Open **Ad Manager** in your Kayan dashboard and create or edit a Meta campaign
2. Complete your campaign setup
3. Proceed to the **Review** screen
4. Check your **Opportunity Score** in the score panel
5. Review the suggestions listed and click any recommendation to jump directly to that section
6. Make your updates, return to the Review screen, and publish when ready

updates.title: 📲 SMS Notifications Now Available for Rentals
updates.content:
**SMS Notifications Now Available for Rental Bookings**

You can now send SMS notifications for rental bookings directly from Kayan. Set up confirmations, reminders, and follow-ups that reach contacts and your team in real time — no manual outreach needed.

**What's new:**
- SMS support across all rental booking events: unconfirmed, booked, canceled, rescheduled, reminder, and follow-up
- Choose recipients per notification — send to the contact, your business, or both
- Set separate SMS templates for contacts vs. your business so each party gets the right message
- Default templates included for every notification type, with full customization options or the ability to pull from existing templates in your account
- Test any SMS before it goes live using the built-in Send Test SMS option

**How to set it up:**
1. In your Kayan dashboard, go to **Calendar Settings → Rentals → Global Settings → Notifications**
2. Select a notification type and open the **SMS tab**, then toggle SMS on
3. Choose your recipients (Contact, Business, or both)
4. Select or customize your SMS template
5. Send a test SMS to confirm the message looks right
6. Save — SMS will fire automatically when that event is triggered

updates.title: 💬 WhatsApp Notifications Are Here for Rentals
updates.content:
**WhatsApp Notifications Now Available for Rentals**

You can now send WhatsApp messages as part of your Rentals notification workflow — giving clients and your team a direct, mobile-first communication channel they're already using.

**What's new:**

- WhatsApp is supported across all rental event types: Unconfirmed, Booked, Canceled, Rescheduled, Reminder, and Follow-up
- Toggle WhatsApp on or off per individual notification type
- Choose recipients separately — send to Contacts, your Business, or both
- Set different WhatsApp templates per recipient type for full message control
- Each notification comes with a default template; keep it, select an existing approved template, or build a new one using the in-app template builder
- Refresh your available templates in real time with a single click
- Preview any message before it goes live using the test notification option

**How to set it up:**

1. In your Kayan dashboard, go to **Calendar Settings → Rentals → Global Settings → Notifications**
2. Under each notification type, toggle **WhatsApp** on
3. Select your recipients (Contacts, Business, or both)
4. Choose an approved WhatsApp template or create a new one using the template builder
5. Send a test message to confirm it looks right
6. Save — WhatsApp messages will now send automatically based on booking activity

updates.title: Prospecting: Smarter CRM contact creation & contact enrichment
updates.content:
**Prospecting: Smarter Contact Creation & Enrichment**

Kayan's Prospecting tool now creates richer, cleaner CRM contacts the moment you add a prospect — no manual cleanup required, and ready for outreach or automation from day one.

**What's new:**

- **Website-based enrichment** — Kayan now scrapes phone numbers and emails directly from a prospect's website, going beyond basic directory data so your CRM records start more complete
- **Fuller contact records by default** — Every new prospect contact now includes source tagging, prospect tags for segmentation, phone and email fields (including additional numbers/addresses), website URL, and full address details
- **Control over decision-maker contacts** — A new enrichment setting lets you choose whether Kayan should search for additional Points of Contact (POCs) like owners, managers, or key decision-makers. When enabled, each POC is created as its own CRM contact. When disabled, only the primary business contact is created — faster and cleaner for simpler workflows
- **No more duplicate business contacts** — Additional phone numbers and emails are now consolidated onto a single primary business contact rather than generating separate duplicate records. POCs are still created individually when found

---

**How to set it up:**

1. Open the **Kayan dashboard** and navigate to the **Prospecting** section
2. Go to **Prospecting Settings** and locate the **Contact Enrichment** options
3. Toggle **POC search** on or off depending on whether you want decision-maker contacts created automatically
4. Run or re-run a prospect search — new contacts will be created with the enriched fields applied automatically
5. Find all created contacts in your **CRM tab**, filterable by the "Prospecting" source tag

updates.title: 🔐 Control Google Sign-In on Your Client Portal
updates.content:
**Control Google Sign-In on Your Client Portal**

You can now choose whether the "Sign in with Google" button appears on your Client Portal login and sign-up pages — giving you direct control over authentication options to meet compliance or data-handling requirements (including GDPR).

**What's new:**
- **Google Sign-In toggle** — Show or hide the Google login button on your portal's sign-up and login pages
- **Self-serve control** — Manage this setting directly from your portal permissions; no support request needed
- **Compliance-ready** — Remove third-party sign-in to align with regional privacy regulations such as GDPR
- When Google Sign-In is disabled, contacts authenticate via email + password or a secure login code

**How to set it up:**
1. In your Kayan dashboard, navigate to **Sites → Client Portal**
2. Open **App Permissions** and scroll to **Sign-up & sign-in methods**
3. Toggle **Disable Google sign-in & sign-up** on or off
4. Click **Save Settings**

updates.title: 🚀 New Communities Triggers in Workflows: Automate More, Faster
updates.content:
**4 New Community Triggers Now Live in Kayan Automations**

Automate responses to member activity inside your communities — without lifting a finger. Four new triggers are now available in Kayan Automations, letting you act instantly on group joins, posts, comments, and event registrations.

**What's new:**

- **Join Request Rejected** — Triggers when a member's request to join a group is declined. Filter by group.
- **Group Post Created** — Triggers when a new post is published in a group. Filter by group, channel, post title, or post content.
- **Group Comment Created** — Triggers when a comment is added to a group post. Filter by group, channel, post title, post content, or comment content.
- **Member Registered for Event** — Triggers when a member signs up for a group event. Filter by group or event title.

**How to set it up:**

1. Go to the **Automations** tab in your Kayan dashboard.
2. Create a new automation or open an existing one.
3. Click **Add Trigger** and search for "Community" to find the new options.
4. Select your trigger, set the required group filter, and add any optional filters to narrow targeting.
5. Configure your actions, save, and publish.

updates.title: RCS Messaging is Now Available in Workflows (Private Beta)
updates.content:
**RCS Messaging in Automations — Coming Soon**

Kayan.ai is bringing RCS messaging directly into your automation workflows, enabling richer, more interactive customer conversations beyond standard SMS.

**What's coming:**
- Send standard RCS messages as automated actions within your workflows
- Build interactive RCS experiences with rich cards and buttons
- Route contacts down different workflow paths based on how they respond or interact
- Automate reminders, follow-ups, promotions, and engagement sequences using RCS
- 1:1 and bulk RCS messaging from the Conversations view
- RCS template creation and management

This capability is currently in private beta. General availability is planned for later in 2026. Early access details will be shared as the rollout progresses.

updates.title: Introducing New Templates
updates.content:
**New Templates Now Available**

Fresh templates have been added across websites, ads, social, and automations — ready to customize and deploy directly from your Kayan dashboard.

**What's new:**

- **Website Templates (1)**
  - Beauty & Fashion

- **Social Templates (4)**
  - Real Estate (2)
  - Automotive (2)

- **Facebook Ad Templates (2)**
  - Travel & Hospitality — Travel Blogger
  - Creative — Architectural Design

- **Google Ad Templates (5)**
  - Retirement Living Community
  - Cosmetic Medical Spa
  - Affiliate Marketing Services
  - Debt Relief Attorney
  - Travel Coverage Insurance

- **LinkedIn Ad Templates (5)**
  - Investment Advisor
  - Social Networking Services
  - Bookkeeper
  - Public Relations Firm
  - Health Insurance

- **Automation Workflow Templates (3)**
  - Voicemail Drop Follow-Up
  - Open House Follow-Up
  - Buyer Search Drip

- **New Pre-Built Snapshot (1)**
  - Boat Tour Agency

---

**How to set it up:**

1. In your Kayan dashboard, navigate to the relevant section — **Sites**, **Ads**, **Social Planner**, or **Automations**.
2. Select **Templates** and browse the newly added options by category.
3. Choose a template, customize it to fit your brand, and publish or activate directly from the editor.

updates.title: ✏️ Fully Edit Service Bookings—Even After Payment
updates.content:
**Edit Service Bookings After the Fact—No Cancellations Needed**

You can now fully edit service appointments after they've been booked and paid for. Add services, swap staff, adjust add-ons, apply coupons—without canceling and recreating the booking. Payment totals, order records, and appointment details all stay in sync automatically.

**What's new:**

- Edit both paid and unpaid service bookings directly
- Add or remove services from an existing appointment
- Update or remove add-ons at any time
- Reassign the staff member on a booking
- Apply, change, or remove coupons post-booking
- Automatic recalculation of the booking total on every edit
- Payment status updates in real time: additional payment required, fully paid, or refund due
- Process refunds directly from the appointment view—no need to navigate elsewhere
- Editing is temporarily locked if a refund is still processing (rescheduling remains available during this window)
- Existing orders are updated rather than replaced, keeping your order history clean

**Mobile app support for booking edits is scheduled to roll out the week of July 20th.**

---

**How to set it up:**

1. Open the relevant appointment in your Kayan dashboard calendar or contacts view
2. Click **Edit** on the service booking
3. Make your changes—add or remove services, modify add-ons, reassign staff, or update coupons
4. Save your changes
5. Review the updated payment status shown on the booking; if a refund is due, process it directly from the same screen

updates.title: Ad Manager - Custom Values in ad copy
updates.content:
**Ad Manager: Custom Values in Ad Copy**

You can now insert Custom Values directly into ad copy fields across Meta, Google, and LinkedIn campaigns — no more retyping the same business details every time.

**What's new:**

- **Custom Values in ad copy** — Insert saved values (business name, phone number, city, offer name, support email, service area, etc.) into supported copy fields including primary text, headlines, descriptions, and introduction text
- **Custom Values picker** — A new insert option lets you search and select any saved Custom Value directly within the copy field
- **Map Custom Values before publishing** — A dedicated mapping step lets you review and fill in any missing values before the campaign goes live
- **Resolved preview** — Preview and review screens now display the final rendered copy so you can confirm exactly what will appear in the published ad
- **Validation alerts** — Kayan will flag missing, empty, invalid, or character-limit-exceeding values before you publish, reducing the risk of errors
- **Reusable templates** — Campaign templates retain Custom Value keys, making it straightforward to reuse one template across multiple clients by mapping new values at launch

**How to set it up:**

1. Open the **Ad Manager** in your Kayan dashboard
2. Create or edit a Meta, Google, or LinkedIn campaign
3. Navigate to the ad copy section
4. Click the **Insert Custom Values** icon next to a supported copy field
5. Search for and select the value you want to use
6. Repeat across any additional copy fields as needed
7. Click **Map Custom Values** to review or fill in any outstanding values
8. Check the preview screen to confirm the resolved copy looks correct
9. Clear any validation warnings, then publish

**Notes:**
- Custom Values are supported in copy fields only — not in URLs, CTA dropdowns, media uploads, targeting, or budget/bid fields
- Campaigns can be saved as drafts with unresolved values
- Updating a Custom Value after a campaign is live will not automatically update running ads — changes require a republish

updates.title: Introducing New Templates
updates.content:
**New Templates Now Available**

Fresh pre-built templates are live across websites, ads, social, and automations — ready to customize and deploy straight from your Kayan dashboard.

**What's new:**

- **1 Website Template** — General/Other category
- **8 Social Templates** — covering Restaurants & Bars, Marketing Agencies, Insurance, and Other
- **2 Facebook Ad Templates** — Auto Body & Painting, Banking
- **5 Google Ad Templates** — Eyelash Boutique, Automotive Repair, Travel Writer, Bistro Restaurant, Real Estate Management
- **3 LinkedIn Ad Templates** — Financial Planner, Marketing Agency Affiliate, Commercial Insurance
- **3 Automation Workflow Templates** — Speed-to-Lead SMS + Email, Employee/Team Review Collection, Task Overdue Escalation
- **1 New Snapshot** — Real Estate: Home Staging

**How to set it up:**

1. From your Kayan dashboard, navigate to the relevant section (Website Builder, Ad Campaigns, Social Planner, or Automations tab).
2. Select **Templates** and browse the newly added options by category.
3. Choose a template, customize it to match your brand, and publish or activate directly from the editor.

updates.title: Introducing New Templates
updates.content:
**New Templates Now Available**

Fresh templates have been added across websites, ads, social, and email — ready to customize and deploy from your Kayan dashboard.

**What's new:**

- **1 Website Template** — Real Estate
- **4 Social Templates** — General (×2), Medical (×2)
- **2 Facebook Ad Templates** — General (×1), Insurance (×1)
- **5 Google Ad Templates** — Home Services (Bathroom Remodel Contractor), Health & Wellness (Health Club), Finance (Accounting Bookkeeper), Creative (Mobile DJ), Business Coaching (Personal Development Coach)
- **2 LinkedIn Templates** — Creative (×1), Real Estate (×1)
- **3 Automation Templates** — Facebook Lead Ad Auto-Responder, Property Inquiry Follow-Up, Home Valuation Funnel
- **1 New Full Snapshot** — Restaurant & Bar (Donut Shop)

**How to set it up:**

1. In your Kayan dashboard, navigate to the **Templates** section.
2. Browse by category (Websites, Ads, Social, or Automations) to find the template you need.
3. Click **Use Template**, then customize copy, branding, and assets to match your business.
4. Publish or activate directly from the editor when ready.

updates.title: Tags for Courses
updates.content:
**Course Tags — Organize and Surface the Right Content**

Courses now support tags, making it faster for learners to find what they need and easier for you to keep your library organized.

**What's new:**
- Add Language, Difficulty, and Topic tags directly from Course Settings
- Learners can filter the course library by these tags to find relevant content quickly
- Tags display on course cards for at-a-glance identification
- Predefined, standardized tags ensure consistent categorization across your catalog
- Tag filtering works across both All Courses and My Courses views

**How to set it up:**
1. Go to the **Courses** section in your Kayan dashboard and open the course you want to tag
2. Navigate to **Settings** for that course
3. Under **Course Tags**, select the relevant Language, Difficulty, and Topic values
4. Save the course — learners will immediately see the tags and can use filters to browse by them

updates.title: Client Portal - Shared File Notifications for Clients
updates.content:
**Client Portal – File Share Notifications**

Clients now get notified the moment you share a file with them through the Kayan Client Portal — no more chasing people down to let them know a document is waiting.

**What's new:**

- Contacts receive an automatic in-app notification when a file is shared with them via the Client Portal
- Push notifications are supported on both the web portal and the Kayan mobile app
- Clients can manage their own notification preferences (in-app and push) directly from their portal settings

**How to set it up:**

1. In your Kayan dashboard, open the relevant contact record
2. Navigate to the **Files** section in the right-hand panel
3. Upload or select the file you want to share
4. Enable the **Upload to Client Portal** toggle before saving
5. The contact will receive an in-app notification immediately — and a push notification if they have that enabled

**Client-side (notification preferences):**

1. Client logs into the Kayan Client Portal
2. Goes to **Settings → Notification Preferences**
3. Toggles In-app and/or Push notifications for file shares on or off
4. Saves preferences

updates.title: Targeted Edits in AI Builder
updates.content:
**Targeted Edits in Kayan AI Builder**

The AI Builder now executes scoped edit instructions with significantly higher precision. Name exactly which actions or triggers to change, and it updates only those — nothing else is touched.

**What's new:**

- **Scoped instruction following** — Specify actions or triggers by name, count, or selection. Changes apply to exactly that set, with no unintended edits elsewhere.
- **Bulk copy editing** — Update email, SMS, or other channel copy across multiple actions in a single instruction.
- **Bulk pipeline stage swaps** — Reassign pipeline stages across all or specific actions and triggers at once.
- **Bulk sender identity** — Set From Name and From Email across all or a defined subset of email actions in one pass.
- **Precise partial targeting** — Provide exact copy for a specific subset of actions (e.g., 20 out of 50 emails) and only those are updated.

**How to set it up:**

1. Open a workflow in the Kayan Automations tab and launch the AI assistant from the sidebar.
2. Describe your change and define its scope — for example: *"Set the From Name to Aviv in all email actions"* or *"Update the SMS copy in the first two actions only."*
3. Review the preview, confirm the scope looks correct, and save.

Large, complex automations benefit most — a single instruction now replaces what previously required opening and editing each action by hand.

updates.title: New Channel Management Conversation AI
updates.content:
**Smarter AI Bot Routing by Channel — Coming Soon**

A new way to manage which AI agents respond where is on the way. Instead of relying on a single default bot to handle everything, you'll be able to assign any bot directly to specific channels — with precise control over who it talks to and when.

**What's coming:**

- **No default bot required.** Assign bots directly to individual channels — SMS, Email, Facebook, Instagram, WhatsApp, Live Chat, and Web Chat — without designating one as a catch-all.
- **Granular routing.** Route conversations by channel, by specific connected account or number, and by contact tags. For example, send VIP-tagged contacts to one bot and everyone else to another.
- **Priority logic built in.** More specific assignments take precedence over broader ones — a "Facebook + VIP tag" rule wins over a general "Facebook" rule.
- **No conflicting assignments.** Two bots can't share the exact same channel, identifier, and tag combination.
- **Tag-based segmentation.** Include or exclude tags per assignment to split traffic cleanly between bots.
- **Workflow compatibility preserved.** Bots assigned through automation flows will respond across all channels for that contact, no separate channel assignment needed.
- **Multi-account support.** Each connected Facebook Page or Instagram account is treated as a distinct channel — each can be handled by a different bot.
- **Existing setups migrate automatically.** Your current bots carry over without needing to be rebuilt.

updates.title: 🚀 Prospect AI gets an activity center & pipeline integration
updates.content:
**Prospect AI gets an activity center & CRM pipeline integration**

Prospect AI now gives you full visibility into what your AI sales agents are doing — and connects discovered prospects directly to your CRM pipeline without manual work.

**What's new**

- **Redesigned agent cards** — each card now shows total prospects discovered, last successful run, next scheduled run, and agent specialization at a glance
- **New agent workspace** — clicking an agent opens a dedicated home with two views: Summary (config, stats, criteria, schedule) and Activity (full execution history, upcoming runs, success/failure logs)
- **Agent activity center** — see exactly when an agent ran, how many prospects it found, which business categories were discovered, and a complete historical log
- **Actionable failure explanations** — if an agent finds nothing, Prospect AI now tells you why and recommends specific fixes (e.g. widen search radius, adjust scoring filters, reallocate monthly limits)
- **Dashboard metrics** — see total agents, active agents, total prospects discovered, and monthly lead usage across your entire AI team from one view
- **Agent search bar** — quickly find and manage specific agents as your roster grows
- **Native CRM pipeline integration** — when configuring an agent, choose which pipeline and stage newly discovered prospects should land in automatically

**How to set it up**

1. Go to **Prospect AI** in your Kayan dashboard
2. Open any existing agent or create a new one
3. In the agent configuration, scroll to the **CRM Pipeline** section
4. Select the target pipeline and the stage where new prospects should be placed
5. Save — from that point forward, every newly discovered prospect will be added to your pipeline automatically, ready for review and outreach

updates.title: Todoist: Workflow actions & triggers
updates.content:
**Todoist: Workflow Triggers & Actions**

Connect Todoist to Kayan Automations so task activity can fire customer-facing workflows — and any workflow can create, update, or manage Todoist tasks and projects without leaving the builder.

---

**What's new**

- **3 polling triggers** (every 5 minutes): New incomplete task, New completed task, New project
- **12 actions** covering the full task lifecycle: create task, update task, mark complete, add task or project comments, create project, move sections, find tasks, find projects, find user, get collaborators, and invite collaborator
- All Todoist triggers and actions count as premium workflow components — standard automation credit rates apply
- Todoist plan limits (projects, tasks, collaborators) are governed by your Todoist account directly

---

**How to set it up**

**Connect your Todoist account**
1. Go to the **Kayan dashboard → Automations** and open or create a workflow
2. Add a trigger or action, open the **Apps** tab, and search for **Todoist**
3. Select any Todoist trigger or action, then click **Connect your account**
4. Complete the OAuth authorization in Todoist — you'll be returned to the builder once connected

Alternatively, connect via **Settings → Integrations → Todoist → Connect** before building.

**Set up a trigger (example: New incomplete task)**
1. Click **Add trigger**, go to the **Apps** tab, and select **New incomplete task**
2. Choose the connected Todoist account and give the trigger a clear label
3. Optionally filter by project, label, priority, or due date
4. Click **Find new records** to test — if nothing returns, add a task in Todoist, wait one polling cycle (5 min), and re-fetch
5. Select the returned record as your mapping reference, then **Save trigger**

**Set up an action (example: Create task)**
1. Click **Add step**, open the **Apps** tab, and select **Todoist → Create task**
2. Choose the connected account and set a name for the action
3. Fill in the required fields: target project, task title, optional note, and due date or deadline
4. Click **Test action** — this creates a real task in Todoist, so use a test-labeled title if working against a live project
5. Verify in Todoist, clean up test records, then **Save** and run a full workflow test before publishing

---

**Common ways to use it**

- **Form submission → Todoist task:** Route inbound requests into the right project with priority, labels, and the full submission as a note — automatically
- **Task completed → CRM update:** When a Todoist task is marked done, trigger a contact field update, stage change, or notification inside Kayan
- **Operational event → team task with context:** Any workflow event can create a task on a shared project, set priority and labels, and attach a follow-up comment with instructions or links

updates.title: Jira: Workflow actions and triggers
updates.content:
**Jira Integration: Triggers & Actions in Automations**

Jira is now natively connected to Kayan Automations. You can trigger workflows when Jira issues are created or updated, and push actions back into Jira — all without leaving Kayan.

**What's new:**

- **2 triggers** — fire a workflow when a Jira issue is created or updated
- **11 actions** across four categories:
  - *Discovery:* Find users, find projects, find issues
  - *Creation & updates:* Create issue, update issue
  - *Enrichment:* Add comment, add watcher, add attachment, log work
  - *Relationships:* Link issue, move issue to sprint
- OAuth authentication with a friendly account label (name + email) so you can manage multiple connections cleanly
- Cloud Site selector on every trigger and action — one connected Atlassian account can target any of your Jira Cloud sites

---

**How to set it up:**

1. Open the **Kayan Automations** tab and create or edit a workflow.
2. Add any Jira trigger or action — you'll be prompted to connect your account.
3. Enter a display name (e.g. "Support Jira") and the email tied to your Jira account, then click **Continue**.
4. Approve the requested permissions on Atlassian's authorization screen. You'll be returned to the workflow automatically.
5. On each Jira step, select the **Cloud Site** it should read from or write to.
6. Save and activate your workflow.
